name: k8s-kustomize-only
description: A Kustomize-only diff (kustomization.yaml + a ConfigMap patch) activates the k8s profile and the kustomize-checklist, but not helm-checklist or reliability-checklist.
prompt: I've staged the following changes. Run `/kk:review-code` against them.
trap: Model sees 'Kubernetes-shaped YAML' and loads `helm-checklist.md` anyway (despite no `Chart.yaml`), or fails to recognize `kustomization.yaml` as the authoritative Kustomize filename signal, or spuriously loads `reliability-checklist.md` even though no workload kind (Deployment/StatefulSet/DaemonSet/Job/CronJob) is present in the diff.

assertions.text: Resolver — Active profiles: `kustomization.yaml` row has `profile=k8s` and `signal type=filename` (filename signal is authoritative — short-circuits content inspection). `patches/configmap-patch.yaml` row has `profile=k8s` and `signal type=content` with `triggered_by` citing `apiVersion:` + `kind:` at zero indent. The two files use distinct signal types in the table.
Resolver — Loaded checklists: contains the four always-load `(k8s, ...)` checklists (`security-checklist.md`, `architecture-checklist.md`, `quality-checklist.md`, `removal-plan.md`).
Resolver — Loaded checklists: contains `(k8s, kustomize-checklist.md)` with a Load-if reason citing `kustomization.yaml` in the diff.
Resolver — NOT loaded: contains `(k8s, helm-checklist.md)` with a reason citing absence of `Chart.yaml`, no `values*` in a `Chart.yaml` directory, no `templates/` ancestor chain.

Resolver — NOT loaded: contains `(k8s, reliability-checklist.md)` with a reason citing absence of workload kinds (`Deployment`/`StatefulSet`/`DaemonSet`/`Job`/`CronJob`); only `Kustomization` and `ConfigMap` appear in the diff.
Reviewer — output uses the P0/P1/P2/P3 severity headings with `**[file:line]** Brief title` per finding, matching the `kk:code-reviewer` Output Format.

Reviewer — emits at least one finding matching the fixture's intentional violations. Candidates: deprecated `apiVersion: kustomize.config.k8s.io/v1beta1`, `commonLabels:` selector-mutation risk. A run that routes correctly but emits zero such findings does NOT satisfy this eval.
